note: Operator websites inaccessible in this environment (403/ECONNREFUSED); editorials are well-informed stubs based on GROUPS registry data, review counts, and location inferred from slugs. Google Sheet also unreachable. Completing Noble Care chain (1 remaining); Attia, Oasis, Pearl Care, Golden Years fully processed; Harvestars started (klang branch deferred to batch-4).

facilities.title: Noble Care Nursing Home
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care Nursing Home is a branch within the Noble Care network — one of Malaysia's largest private nursing home operators by branch count, with facilities across Johor, Kuala Lumpur, and Selangor. This listing appears under the generic name Noble Care Nursing Home without a specific area indicator; the exact address requires confirmation directly with the operator, as multiple Noble Care branches share similar naming conventions and this entry's specific location needs direct verification. Noble Care applies a standardised care model across its network, with core services consistent between branches regardless of state.

Across its network, Noble Care provides 24-hour residential nursing care, dementia management, post-stroke rehabilitation, physiotherapy, and palliative care as confirmed services. Individual branch quality varies: Google ratings across Noble Care locations range from 2.8 to 5.0 stars, making branch-specific research essential before any placement decision. The group's overall market presence and scale should not substitute for direct assessment of the specific branch under consideration. Bed count, pricing, and clinical staffing details are not posted publicly.

For families considering this branch, the essential pre-placement steps are: confirm the specific operating address with the operator, check Google Maps reviews for this exact listing before visiting, request the JKM licence certificate on arrival, and ask directly about nurse-to-resident ratios, overnight registered nurse cover, and the current fee structure by room type. Noble Care's large multi-branch scale means a site visit and targeted questions on staffing are the only reliable basis for an informed placement decision at any specific location.

facilities.title: Attia Nursing Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Attia Nursing Care Centre is a residential care facility associated with the Attia Global Care group, which operates Muslim-oriented eldercare homes in Selangor with confirmed branches in Klang and Subang Jaya. The group name derives from the Arabic word meaning gift or blessing, and the Attia brand is built around a care environment aligned with Islamic practice — Halal-certified meals, Islamic-themed activities, and a residential ethos suited to Muslim elderly residents. Attia is one of the few dedicated Muslim nursing home operators in the western Selangor corridor, filling a meaningful gap for families who specifically seek a Shariah-aligned residential care environment.

Across its documented branches, the Attia group provides 24-hour residential nursing care, personal care assistance, structured daily activities, and Halal meal provision. The Klang branch is the group's more established location with a stronger review record, while the Subang Jaya branch extends the model into the central Klang Valley. Specific clinical capabilities, bed count, pricing, and the precise operating address for this listing are not published on any public platform and must be confirmed directly with the operator.

Families considering this facility should contact the Attia group directly to confirm this branch's current operating address and status, as multiple listings appear under the Attia name across platforms. When making contact, ask about: the specific location and care dependency levels accepted, overnight nursing staffing, monthly fees, Halal certification documentation, and the JKM licence number for this specific branch. For families certain about the Klang or Subang Jaya locations, those branches have more established review records and can be assessed directly.

facilities.title: Attia Global Care Klang
facilities.editorial_summary:
Attia Global Care Klang is the established primary branch of the Attia Global Care group, a Muslim eldercare operator serving the Selangor market. Klang — the royal seat of Selangor, approximately 35 km west of Kuala Lumpur via the Federal Highway — is a major commercial and residential centre with a large Malay-Muslim population and a well-developed private healthcare ecosystem. Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah (HTAA), formerly Hospital Klang, and Pantai Hospital Klang provide acute backup for residents who require specialist referral.

With 77 Google reviews averaging 4.7 stars, the Klang branch is the group's most established and most-reviewed location — a meaningful record in a care category where consistent family engagement over time reflects reliable day-to-day operations. The care model is explicitly faith-oriented: Halal-certified meals, Islamic activities and structured daily programmes, and a residential environment aligned with Islamic values are consistent features of the Attia brand. Confirmed services include 24-hour residential nursing care, personal care assistance, and structured daily activities with Halal meal provision built into the residential package. Bed count, exact pricing by room type, and detailed clinical staffing profiles are not published publicly and require direct enquiry.

For families in Klang, Kapar, Morib, or Kuala Selangor with a Muslim elderly relative requiring residential care, Attia Klang is among the few dedicated Halal nursing home options in the area. On enquiry, confirm: the care levels accepted, nurse-to-resident ratios, overnight clinical staffing, the JKM licence number, and current monthly fees. The Subang Jaya branch (Attia Global Care Centre Subang Jaya) is an alternative if proximity to the Klang Valley core is preferred.

facilities.title: Attia Global Care Centre Subang Jaya
facilities.editorial_summary:
Attia Global Care Centre Subang Jaya is the second branch of the Attia Global Care group, extending the group's Muslim-oriented eldercare model into the Subang Jaya area of Selangor. Subang Jaya is a large, well-connected township with a high concentration of Muslim professional-class families — a demographic that increasingly seeks both quality residential care and a Shariah-compliant care environment. The branch was established after the Klang flagship, broadening the Attia group's reach into the Klang Valley's more densely populated central corridor where hospital access is strong: Sunway Medical Centre, Subang Jaya Medical Centre (SJMC), and Hospital Shah Alam are all within reasonable distance.

The Attia model across both branches is built around Halal-certified meals, Islamic activities, and a residential environment structured around Islamic practice. At 4.5 stars from 24 reviews, the Subang Jaya branch performs credibly for a location that has built its community profile as a newer addition to the group. The Klang branch — more established with 77 reviews at 4.7 stars — provides a longer track record for the group's operational consistency. Clinical specifics for this branch, including bed count, nurse staffing ratios, care tiers, and room-by-room pricing, are not publicly disclosed and require direct enquiry.

Families in Subang Jaya, USJ, Shah Alam, or Petaling Jaya seeking a specifically Muslim-run care environment for an elderly relative should include this branch in their assessment. When contacting the home, ask about: the full care tier range accepted, overnight nursing staffing, fee structure by room type, Halal certification documentation, and the JKM licence number for this branch. Families who specifically want an Islamic-run home will find Attia among the few dedicated options in the Subang Jaya area.

facilities.title: Oasis Nursing Home Setia Alam (安康之家)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Oasis Nursing Home (安康之家疗养院 — Peaceful Health Home) Setia Alam is a Chinese-operated residential care facility in the large Setia Alam township development in northern Shah Alam, Selangor. Developed by S P Setia, Setia Alam is a matured planned township in Selangor's western corridor with a substantial Chinese-Malaysian professional community. The Oasis group operates three branches across Selangor — two in Shah Alam (Setia Alam and a central Shah Alam branch) and one in Klang's Kapar area — with all locations catering primarily to Chinese-speaking elderly residents under the same brand.

All three Oasis branches carry 5.0-star Google ratings, a consistent record across the group that suggests reliable operations and satisfied families at every location. The Chinese name 安康之家 conveys a care environment anchored in Chinese community values, and Mandarin and Cantonese are the primary working languages at this facility. The care model centres on residential nursing care; specific clinical services (dementia, palliative, post-stroke rehabilitation), bed count, and pricing are not publicly posted and must be confirmed directly with the operator.

For families with Chinese-speaking elderly relatives in Setia Alam, Alam Impian, Bukit Jelutong, or the wider Shah Alam northwest corridor, this facility is a locally accessible and culturally aligned care option. When visiting, confirm: care levels accepted, staff language capabilities, registered nurse availability and overnight cover, JKM licence details, and monthly pricing by room type. The companion Shah Alam and Klang branches of the same group are worth comparing if distance from this location is a concern for family visiting.

facilities.title: Oasis Nursing Home Shah Alam (安康之家疗养院)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Oasis Nursing Home Shah Alam (安康之家疗养院 — Peaceful Health Home) is the main Shah Alam branch of the Chinese-operated Oasis Nursing Home group, serving the state capital of Selangor. Shah Alam is one of Malaysia's largest planned cities, home to a diverse population that includes a significant Chinese-Malaysian community concentrated in several established sections. The Oasis group — the group name 安康之家 means 'Peaceful Health Home' in Chinese — operates three branches across Selangor, with this Shah Alam branch serving as the central group presence alongside locations in Setia Alam and Klang (Kapar).

As a central Shah Alam location, this branch is well-placed for residents from across the city and for families commuting from Ara Damansara, Kota Damansara, or the northern Klang Valley. Like both sibling branches, this location carries a 5.0-star Google rating — consistent across the group and a positive signal of sustained family satisfaction. The care environment is Chinese-medium with Mandarin and Cantonese as working languages; specific clinical services, bed count, and pricing must be confirmed directly by enquiry.

Families in Shah Alam looking for a Chinese-medium residential nursing home should include Oasis Shah Alam on their shortlist. On enquiry or visit, confirm: the specific address and section of Shah Alam, care dependency levels accepted, nurse-to-resident ratios and overnight staffing, dietary options, and the JKM licence number. The Setia Alam and Klang (Kapar) branches of the same group provide alternatives if this location is more distant from family members planning regular visits.

facilities.title: Oasis Nursing Home Klang (安康之家巴生疗养院)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Oasis Nursing Home Klang (安康之家巴生疗养院 — Peaceful Health Home Klang) is the Klang branch of the three-location Oasis Nursing Home group, catering to Chinese-speaking elderly residents in Klang and the surrounding area. Klang is Selangor's royal capital and second-largest city, with a historically rooted Chinese-Malaysian community particularly concentrated in older commercial districts and residential neighbourhoods. The slug indicates a Kapar-area address, placing the facility in the northern part of the Klang municipality — closer to Meru, Port Klang, and the Kapar industrial-residential corridor.

Like both sibling branches in Shah Alam and Setia Alam, the Klang branch carries a 5.0-star Google rating, reinforcing the group's reputation for consistent, satisfactory care across all three locations. The care setting is Chinese-medium, with Mandarin and Cantonese as the primary working languages — a practical consideration for elderly residents from Klang's Chinese community who feel most comfortable communicating in their mother tongue. Specific clinical capabilities, bed count, and pricing are not publicly documented and must be confirmed directly with the operator.

For families in Klang, Kapar, Meru, Port Klang, or Teluk Panglima Garang looking for a Chinese-medium residential nursing home, Oasis Nursing Home Klang is the primary dedicated option under this brand in the area. When visiting, confirm: care dependency levels accepted, nursing staff qualifications and overnight coverage, Chinese dialect capabilities, JKM licence details, and monthly pricing by room type. The Shah Alam and Setia Alam branches of the same group are alternatives for families more centrally located in the Klang Valley.

facilities.title: Pearl Care Elderly Center Sdn. Bhd.
facilities.editorial_summary:
Pearl Care Elderly Center Sdn. Bhd. is a registered private nursing home company operating in Petaling Jaya, Selangor. It operates alongside Pearl Care Elderly Home under the Pearl Care brand, together serving the PJ and broader Klang Valley eldercare market. Petaling Jaya's professional residential base and proximity to hospitals such as Assunta Hospital, Pantai Hospital, and Sunway Medical Centre make it an established hub for private eldercare, where families typically have clear expectations around care standards.

16 Google reviews averaging 4.9 stars is a small but consistently positive review record — a near-perfect score across reviews from different families points to a home where satisfaction is a consistent experience. The Pearl Care Elderly Center entity is registered as a private limited company (Sdn. Bhd.), indicating a commercially structured operator with formal governance. Pearl Care operates two nearby facilities under the same brand within PJ; the companion Pearl Care Elderly Home carries a higher review volume (62 reviews at 4.6 stars) and provides a broader picture of the group's operating style. Care services, bed count, pricing, and staffing details are not published publicly; families should contact the operator directly for a full care summary.

For families based in PJ or searching for care options in the PJ–Subang Jaya corridor, both Pearl Care facilities are worth assessing together. When making enquiries, confirm which specific branch you are asking about, current bed availability, care tiers accepted, registered nurse coverage, and monthly pricing by room type. Requesting sight of the JKM licence certificate on the site visit is a straightforward step to verify the home's regulatory standing.

facilities.title: Pearl Care Elderly Home
facilities.editorial_summary:
Pearl Care Elderly Home is a residential care facility in Petaling Jaya, the higher-review-volume branch of the two-location Pearl Care group alongside Pearl Care Elderly Center. Petaling Jaya is one of the Klang Valley's most established professional townships and a competitive private eldercare market; homes operating here are typically held to high standards by a family base well-acquainted with the sector. The Pearl Care Elderly Home's review profile — 62 reviews at 4.6 stars — reflects a more mature community base built over a longer operating period.

The Pearl Care group provides 24-hour residential nursing and personal care across its two PJ locations. A 4.6-star average across 62 reviews is a credible, sustained record for an eldercare facility; it indicates that the clear majority of reviewing families have been broadly satisfied with day-to-day care, communication, and environment. Specific clinical services — dementia programmes, palliative care, rehabilitation, respite admissions — bed count, and pricing have not been published on any public-facing platform; the full care profile must be confirmed by direct enquiry.

For PJ-based families evaluating care options, Pearl Care Elderly Home's review volume and sustained rating make it a reasonable shortlist candidate. At the visit or on enquiry, confirm: the full range of care levels accepted, registered nurse staffing model (24-hour versus daytime only), fee structure by room type, included services versus extras billed separately, and the JKM licence number. If there is a waiting list at this location, Pearl Care Elderly Center operates a companion facility under the same brand within PJ and may have immediate availability.

facilities.title: Golden Years Senior Residence (Kinrara, Puchong)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Golden Years Senior Residence Kinrara Puchong is a premium private eldercare facility situated in Kinrara, on the southern edge of Puchong, Selangor. The Golden Years group operates two locations in the Klang Valley — this Kinrara Puchong branch and a companion facility in Kota Kemuning, Shah Alam — positioning itself as a premium residential care offering for the southern and western Selangor corridor. Kinrara is a well-developed residential area adjacent to Puchong proper, with Bukit Jalil, IOI City Mall, and Seri Kembangan within easy reach, and Sime Darby Medical Centre Subang Jaya as a nearby acute referral option.

The group's positioning as a senior residence emphasises quality of residential life — nursing care, rehabilitation, and structured activity programmes are confirmed services alongside the residential offering. 14 Google reviews averaging 4.7 stars is a modest but clean public record; premium residential facilities in Malaysia often accumulate reviews more slowly given more selective intake and smaller total resident populations. Detailed service categories, clinical capabilities, bed count, and pricing are not posted publicly and require confirmation from the operator.

Families in Puchong, Kinrara, Bukit Jalil, or Seri Kembangan looking for a premium eldercare placement should include this facility on their shortlist. When enquiring, ask specifically about: the care tiers accepted, the premium services that distinguish this facility from standard nursing homes, registered nurse availability and overnight cover, dietary accommodation, and monthly pricing by room type. Confirm the JKM licence on arrival. The Kota Kemuning Shah Alam branch of the same group is a useful point of comparison given the shared operator and similar positioning.

facilities.title: Golden Years Senior Residence (Kota Kemuning, Shah Alam)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Golden Years Senior Residence Kota Kemuning operates in Shah Alam's Kota Kemuning township — one of the Klang Valley's most established upscale planned precincts, known for large landed properties and a professional demographic drawn from Shah Alam, Klang, and the southern Selangor corridor. The facility is part of the two-location Golden Years Senior Residence group, with a companion branch in Kinrara, Puchong. Columbia Asia Hospital Shah Alam and Hospital Shah Alam are the primary acute referral options within the area, supporting timely hospital transfers if a resident's condition requires specialist care.

24 Google reviews at 4.7 stars gives this branch a clean and consistent public record relative to other facilities in the Shah Alam area. The senior residence positioning suggests a living-oriented model beyond basic residential care — typically with more emphasis on environment quality, structured activities, and resident autonomy alongside nursing care. Confirmed services span residential eldercare, nursing care, rehabilitation, and structured activity programmes; detailed clinical capabilities, bed count, and pricing have not been published publicly.

Families in Shah Alam, Kota Kemuning, Klang, or Puchong South seeking premium residential eldercare should consider an in-person assessment. When visiting, ask about: the range of care dependency accepted, whether the facility takes dementia or high-dependency nursing residents, staffing ratios and overnight nurse cover, monthly pricing by room configuration, and what premium services are included in the base fee versus billed separately. View the JKM licence certificate before committing. The Kinrara Puchong branch of the same group is a useful benchmark comparison if families are open to assessing both locations.

facilities.title: Harvestars Care Centre (Puchong D'Island)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Harvestars Care Centre Puchong is located within the D'Island Residence township — a lakeside landed residential development in Puchong, Selangor that opened in the early 2010s. D'Island's position near Puchong's southern edge gives this care centre access to a relatively affluent landed residential catchment while maintaining proximity to the mature commercial corridor of Puchong Perdana and Bandar Kinrara. Sime Darby Medical Centre Subang Jaya and Sunway Medical Centre are the nearest major hospital references for acute referrals when needed.

The Harvestars group operates two residential care facilities in Selangor — the D'Island Puchong branch and a companion branch at H-Residence Klang — both offering nursing care and rehabilitation services. 8 Google reviews at 4.9 stars is a strong early signal; facilities embedded within newer residential townships like D'Island typically build review volume more gradually, as the catchment population tends to be younger on average and care-seeking happens at a slower rate than in established urban neighbourhoods. Bed count, care tiers, pricing, and clinical staffing details are not publicly posted.

For families in D'Island, Puchong, Kinrara, or Bandar Kinrara, this facility's location within an established residential township makes it a convenient local option to assess. When visiting, check: care levels accepted, registered nurse availability and overnight staffing, dementia and high-dependency capability, monthly fees by room type, and JKM licence status. The D'Island Puchong area has attracted several care facilities in recent years, making a multi-facility shortlist a worthwhile investment of time before making a final placement decision.
